items.title: Hollinger POTD Raymond Felton. UNC PG was drafted 5th by CHA in 2005. Earned starting job as rook & held it thru 2010. Peaked at 15.5 ppg 8.3 rpg in 2010-11. Also good for NY in 2012-13. Solid player but never a star. One of the better husky short NBA PGs. Retired in 2019.
items.author: HoopsAnalyst
items.body_excerpt: Hollinger POTD Raymond Felton. UNC PG was drafted 5th by CHA in 2005. Earned starting job as rook & held it thru 2010. Peaked at 15.5 ppg 8.3 rpg in 2010-11. Also good for NY in 2012-13. Solid player but never a star. One of the better husky short NBA PGs. Retired in 2019.

items.title: Dominant from the Fever. Their lead was in the mid 30s at one point, and they roll to a 111-87 win over the Sparks. Kelsey Mitchell with an easy 26 points. Season-best performance from Ty Harris. Fever are 11-8 with 10 days before their next game.
items.author: Tony East
items.body_excerpt: Dominant from the Fever. Their lead was in the mid 30s at one point, and they roll to a 111-87 win over the Sparks. Kelsey Mitchell with an easy 26 points. Season-best performance from Ty Harris. Fever are 11-8 with 10 days before their next game.

items.title: Fever cruise to a 111-87 win over the Sparks — as good as it gets before enjoying a week between games.
items.author: Scott Agness
items.body_excerpt:
Fever cruise to a 111-87 win over the Sparks — as good as it gets before enjoying a week between games.

11 Fever players scored. Four starters with 15+, led by Kelsey Mitchell's 26. They finished with shooting %: 55-53-82.

Next: July 5 at Las Vegas

items.title: The 236 combined points allowed by the Los Angeles Sparks over the last two games are the most ever in WNBA history, and it marks the FOURTH(!!!!) time the league record has been broken in the last week.
items.author: Justin Russo
items.body_excerpt: The 236 combined points allowed by the Los Angeles Sparks over the last two games are the most ever in WNBA history, and it marks the FOURTH(!!!!) time the league record has been broken in the last week.

One day before her 23rd birthday, Bree Hall gets her first minutes of the season. Second time being activated as a development player.

The Ohio native came right in and knocked down a 3-pointer.

Fever cruising vs. the Sparks, +31 after 3.

items.title: Fever handling the Sparks, up 57-37 at halftime. They shot 58% and made 15/17 FTs. Good ball movement and decisive attacks. Also key: just six turnovers.
items.author: Scott Agness
items.body_excerpt:
Fever handling the Sparks, up 57-37 at halftime. They shot 58% and made 15/17 FTs. Good ball movement and decisive attacks. Also key: just six turnovers.

Boston with 17pts, Mitchell and Harris each have 11.

items.title: Dominant first half for the Fever. They're up 57-37 at the break thanks to a monster 30-16 second quarter. Aliyah Boston already has 17 points and 8 rebounds.
items.author: Tony East
items.body_excerpt: Dominant first half for the Fever. They're up 57-37 at the break thanks to a monster 30-16 second quarter. Aliyah Boston already has 17 points and 8 rebounds.

Aliyah Boston, Ty Harris, and Monique Billings: 30 points
The Sparks: 31 points

It's 48-31 Fever. Fever are dominating. Timeout, LA.
